properties.remark: A MUD SLIDE RESULTED IN THE TEMPORARY CLOSURE OF TWO LANES ON INTERSTATE 15 NEAR DEVORE. STORM TOTAL RAINFALL AS OF 8 AM AT DEVORE WAS 5.25 INCHES.

PRELIMINARY REPORTS INDICATE THAT A VEHICLE WITH TWO OCCUPANTS WAS SWEPT DOWNSTREAM INTO MILL CREEK AFTER THE DRIVER WENT AROUND A BARRICADE AT CHINO CORONA ROAD NEAR PRADO DAM. ONE OCCUPANT WAS FOUND HYPOTHERMIC AND CLINGING TO A TREE. THE VEHICLE AND ITS SECOND OCCUPANT ARE STILL MISSING.
A SINKHOLE HAS OPENED UP ON BRYANT STREET IN WILDOMAR NEAR GRAND AVENUE. ROADWAY FLOODING IS CONCEALING THE HOLE AND AT LEAST ONE VEHICLE HAS BECOME STUCK.
BLOWING SAND ALONG INTERSATE 10 FROM BEAUMONT TO THE CALIFORNIA/ARIZONA STATE LINE.

LIVE OAK CANYON RD IS CLOSED DUE TO MULTIPLE FLASH FLOODS. ROADWAY IN UNDER 4 FEET OF WATER.

SECOND FLASH FLOOD OCCURRING AT LIVE OAK CANYON ROAD NEAR ONIELL PARK. HORSE POSSIBLY BEING EVACUATED. ROAD SHUT DOWN. ROAD CREWS STUCK IN FLASH FLOODING OR MUDSLIDES. INJURIES REPORTED BUT NO SPECIFIC NUMBERS GIVEN. LOTS OF MUD AND DEBRIS ON THE ROADWAY IN MULTIPLE PLACES.

*** 1 INJ *** HEAVY RAIN FROM A THUNDERSTORM OVER THE FOOTHILLS ABOVE CATHEDRAL CITY PUSHED TONS OF DIRT FROM A CONSTRUCTION SITE INTO SOUTH PORTIONS OF THE CITY FLOODING A TRAILER PARK AND AN AUTO DEALERSHIP ALONG WITH 15 TO 20 OTHER BUSINESSES. SEVERAL CARS WERE DAMAGED AND SOME RESIDENTS OF THE TRAILER PARK WERE EVACUATED. ABOUT 3 TO 3.5 FT OF MUD AND WATER COVERED NEARBY ROADS MAKING THEM IMPASSABLE. SIX PEOPLE WERE EVACUATED TO A RED CROSS SHELTER. ONE MINOR INJURY. SEVERAL POWER POLES WERE KNOCKED OVER.
MUD AND SAND WASHED ACROSS COUNTY ROAD S2 AT SEVERAL LOCATIONS IN SHELTER VALLEY, BLAIR VALLEY, BOX CANYON, AND MASON VALLEY...BETWEEN SCISSORS JUNCTION AND SALT CREEK CROSSING AT MILE MARKER 32.
URBAN FLOODING. 1.25 INCHES OF RAIN SINCE 1415 PDT. VISIBILITY 1/4 MILE OR LESS.
DIME TO NICKEL SIZE HAIL IN SOUTH LA QUINTA COVE AREA. HEAVY RAIN, 1.25 INCHES, IN LAST HOUR CAUSING URBAN FLOODING AND VISIBILITY A QUARTER MILE OR LESS. FREQUENT CLOUD TO GROUND LIGHTNING.

SAN FELIPE CREEK FLOODING COUNTY HIGHWAY S2 AT MILE MARKER 4.

HIGHWAY 78 FLOODED BETWEEN SHELTER VALLEY AND YAQUI PASS AT SAN FELIPE CREEK CROSSING
FLASH FLOOD DEBRIS FLOW IN THE FERN VALLEY AREA. SIX TO EIGHT INCHES OF DEBRIS COVERED THE ROADS IN THE FERN VALLEY AREA NORTHEAST OF IDYLLWILD.
FLASH FLOODING WAS REPORTED IN THE CAMPGROUND AT LAKE HEMET BY A CAMPGROUND EMPLOYEE. SMALL HAIL AND STRONG WINDS WERE ALSO REPORTED. HIGHWAY 74 WAS REPORTED TO BE FLOODED IN THE LAKE HEMET AREA.

SAN BERNARDINO COUNTY FIRE REPORTS 7 PEOPLE WERE TRAPPED IN THE MIDDLE OF MILL CREEK. THEY WERE ON HIGH GROUND SURROUNDED BY 2 TO 3 FEET OF FLOWING WATER AND DEBRIS, INCLUDING BOULDERS AND LOGS. SAN BERNARDINO COUNTY OFFICIALS WERE ON SITE, AND ADVISED THE GROUP TO REMAIN WHERE THEY WERE UNTIL THE WATER RECEDED TO ITS NORMAL DEPTH OF 6 TO 8 INCHES.
WIND GUST WAS FROM OUTFLOW FROM THUNDERSTORMS ABOUT 30 MILES TO THE SOUTHEAST.
PHOTO TAKEN FROM LUCERNE VALLEY FIRE STATION LOOKING EAST SHOWS LANDSPOUT TORNADO ON THE GROUND. OBSERVERS ESTIMATED DURATION 3-5 MINUTES.
PHOTO TAKEN FROM LUCERNE VALLEY FIRE STATION LOOKING EAST SHOWS TORNADO ON THE GROUND. OBSERVERS ESTIMATED DURATION 3-5 MINUTES.
FLASH FLOOD AND DEBRIS FLOW WITHIN THE 2007 SANTIAGO BURN AREA. AREA IMPACTED WAS IN MODJESKA CANYON. MUD ROCKS AND FLOOD WATERS MOVED ACROSS ROADS AND THE BACKYARD OF A HOUSE IN THE CANYON

.91 INCHES OF RAIN SINCE MIDNIGHT IN LA QUINTA/INDIO AREA. QUARTER INCH RAIN RATE SINCE 9 AM. FLASH FLOODING/URBAN FLOODING FROM HEAVY RAINS IN INDIO. *** 3 INJ *** TIJUANA RIVER FLOODING, 21 PEOPLE RESCUED, ALONG WITH 70 HORSES AND OTHER LIVESTOCK. THREE PEOPLE HOSPITALIZED FOR HYPOTHERMIA. SEVERAL LIVESTOCK DROWNED.
MOTORIST DROVE VEHICLE AROUND BARRICADES INTO A FLOODED INTERSECTION, GOT STRANDED AND HAD TO BE RESCUED.
APARTMENT ROOF COLLAPSED UNDER THE WEIGHT OF ACCUMULATED RAINFALL.
